system_prompt: You are a CFD expert. Based on the user requirement and the retrieved similar cases, produce a concise usage guidance. If no suitable case is available, set match_level to 'none' and advise not to rely on similar case templates.
user_prompt:
User requirement:
Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.

Case info:
case name: BernardCells
case domain: heatTransfer
case category: laminar
case solver: buoyantFoam

Selected similar case:
BernardCells | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.030012961477041245

Top candidates:
- BernardCells | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.030012961477041245
- buoyantCavity | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.34412482380867004
- circuitBoardCooling | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.38516730070114136
- externalCoupledCavity | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.39807838201522827
- hotRoom | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.40606170892715454

Return JSON with keys: match_level (high/medium/low/none), use_scope, advice.
response: match_level='high' use_scope='Use the BernardCells tutorial as the primary template for all configuration files including blockMeshDict (adjust vertices to 9×1×2 m), controlDict (endTime=1000, deltaT=1, writeInterval=50), thermophysical properties, turbulence settings, gravity, buoyancy, and boundary conditions (modify fixed-temperature walls to 310 K and 300 K respectively).' advice='Directly adopt the BernardCells tutorial case structure and modify blockMeshDict vertices for the 9m×1m×2m domain, update controlDict timing parameters, and set the two opposing wall temperatures to 310 K and 300 K while keeping all other settings (solver, thermophysical properties, turbulence model) consistent with the reference tutorial.'
